title: Roadmap derives from human-vouched PROPOSED decisions; auto-drafts route to review
context: The system needed a real-time 'what's next' view that stays in sync with decisions without manual maintenance. Extracted/LLM-generated proposals would otherwise clutter the roadmap with work-done-already signals.
decision: The NOW page roadmap shows only PROPOSED decisions where provenance.source includes 'human_confirmed', not auto-drafted proposals. Un-vouched drafts are counted separately and route to the `hunch review` queue instead. Shipping a roadmap item (accept/supersede its decision) automatically removes it from the roadmap with zero file maintenance.
consequences: Roadmap entries are automatically pruned when decisions ship — no stale plan removal needed.
Auto-drafted proposals (extracted, llm_draft) stay off roadmap; they surface in review queue instead.
Single source of truth: decision graph status="proposed" + provenance, eliminating sync debt.
Requires human confirmation to appear as planned work — raises signal-to-noise on what's actually intended.
alternatives_rejected: Separate roadmap document — requires manual sync with decision graph, risk of divergence.
Show all PROPOSED decisions including auto-drafts — drowns real plans in extracted/synthesized noise.
Show only auto-drafted proposals on roadmap — loses visibility into intentional next work.

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Separate roadmap document — requires manual sync with decision graph, risk of divergence.
Show all PROPOSED decisions including auto-drafts — drowns real plans in extracted/synthesized noise.
Show only auto-drafted proposals on roadmap — loses visibility into intentional next work.
